author: śrī satyavrata muni)
verses:
namāmīśvaraṁ saccidānanda⋅rūpaṁ
lasat⋅kuṇḍalaṁ gokule bhrājamānam
jaśodā⋅bhiyolūkhalāddhāvamānaṁ
parāmṛṣṭamatyantatato drutya gopyā

rudantaṁ muhurnetra⋅yugmaṁ mṛjantaṁ
karāmbhoja⋅yugmena sātaṅka⋅netram
muhuḥśvāsa⋅kampattri⋅rekhāṅka⋅kaṇṭha
sthita⋅graiva⋅dāmodaraṁ bhakti⋅baddham

itīdṛk sva⋅līlābhirānanda⋅kuṇḍe
sva⋅ghoṣaṁ nimajjantamākhyāpayantam
tadīyeśita-jñeṣu bhaktairjitattvaṁ
punaḥ prematastaṁ śatāvṛtti vande

varaṁ deva! mokṣaṁ na mokṣāvadhiṁ vā
na cānyaṁ vṛṇe‛haṁ vareśādapīha
idante vapurnātha! gopāla⋅bālaṁ
sadā me manasyāvirāstāṁ kima-nyaiḥ

idamte mukhāmbhojamavyakta⋅nīlai⋅
rvṛtaṁ kuntalaiḥ snigdha⋅raktaiśca gopyā
muhuścumbitaṁ bimba⋅raktādharaṁ me
manasyāvirāstāma-laṁ lakṣa⋅lābhaiḥ

namo deva! dāmodarānanta! viṣṇo!
prasīda prabho! duḥkha⋅jālāvdhi⋅magnam
kṛpādṛṣṭi⋅vṛṣṭyātidīnaṁ vatānu⋅
gṛhāṇeśa! māmajñamakṣyakṣi⋅dṛśyaḥ

kuverātmajau baddha⋅mūrtyaiva jadvat
tvayā mocitau bhakti⋅bhājau kṛtau ca
tathā prema⋅bhaktiṁ svakāṁ me prayaccha
na mokṣe graho me‛sti dāmodareha

namaste‛stu dāmne sphuraddīpti⋅dhāmne
tvadīyodarāyātha viśvasya dhāmne
namo rādhikāyai tvadīya⋅priyāyai
namo‛nanta⋅līlāya devāya tubhyam
en_translation:
I bow down to that Supreme Controller, whose form is eternal bliss and knowledge, whose ears are adorned with swinging earrings, and who is splendidly manifest in Gokula. He is fleeing from Mother Yaśodā due to fear, being pursued by her to the point of being caught.

He is crying again and again, rubbing His two eyes with His two lotus-like hands, His eyes filled with fear. His neck, marked with three lines, is trembling due to His frequent breaths, and the garland around His belly is tied by the devotion of Yaśodā.

To this Lord, who immerses His own people in pools of joy through such pastimes and who reveals that He is conquered by His devotees who possess knowledge of His supremacy, I offer my obeisances again and again with love.

O Lord! I do not pray for liberation, nor for its ultimate limit, nor any other boon from the bestowers of boons. O Lord! May Your form as a cowherd child always be manifest in my mind. What need is there for anything else?

O Lord! May Your lotus-like face—encircled by dark-blue, soft, curly hair and repeatedly kissed by Mother Yaśodā, with reddish bimba fruit-like lips—always be manifest in my mind. What is the use of millions of other gains?

O Lord! I offer my obeisances to You, O Dāmodara! O Ananta! O Viṣṇu! Be pleased, O Master! I am submerged in a network of miseries. O Lord! Please bestow Your merciful glance upon me, who am extremely poor and ignorant. Become visible before my eyes!

Just as the two sons of Kuvera, though bound in material forms, were liberated by You and made into devotees, similarly, please bestow upon me Your own loving devotion. I have no desire for liberation in this world, O Dāmodara!

My obeisances to the rope which is a brilliant abode! And to Your belly, which is the abode of the entire universe! My obeisances to Rādhikā, Your beloved! My obeisances to You, O Lord, whose pastimes are endless!
